rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PETERSON et al VS. ALFONSO CASTRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT ROARK O'NEILL Amended Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs. The matter is continued to January 23, 2015 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 6, 2015 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The Court has courtesy copies of the Amended Notice of Motion but none of the actual moving papers. The Court also has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DGP ASSOCIATES LP VS. AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF DGP ASSOCIATES LP Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. The Motion is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff is granted leave to file its Proposed First Amended Complaint (FAC). (See CCP § 473(a)(1); see also Guidery v. Green, 95 Cal. 630, 633 [rarely is a court "justified in refusing a party LTA his pleadings so that he may properly present his case"]. Defendant has not argued that Plaintiff's Motion is untimely or that it will suffer any prejudice if the Motion is granted. (Morgan v. Sup. Ct. of Calif. (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530 ["If the motion to amend is timely made and the granting of the motion will not prejudice the opposing party, it is error to refuse permission to amend . . . ."].) Plaintiff's requests to have the Proposed FAC deemed filed and served and the original Answers deemed the Answers to the FAC are denied. Plaintiff must file and serve the FAC pursuant to statute and local rules, and Defendants are entitled to file any responsive pleadings permitted by statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HOMAYUN ZADEH VS. JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Pltff To Appear At Deposition And Produce Documents; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 6, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY, JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. Motion To Compel Pltff To Appear At Deposition And Produce Documents; And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel plaintiff to appear at deposition and produce documents is granted. Plaintiff to appear at his deposition with documents responsive to the deposition notice on a mutually agreed upon date no later than February 6, 2015. If the parties cannot agree on a mutually agreed upon date and time then the deposition shall be held at the law offices of Bryan Cave, 560 Mission Street, 25th Floor, San Francisco on February 6, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. and will proceed pursuant to the Code of Civil Procedure. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted against plaintiff and his attorney of record, Timothy Y. Fong. Sanctions in the amount of $3238.80 to be paid to Defendant and their attorneys of record no later than 9:30 a.m. February 6, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants' Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, Nos. 104, 105, 111, 118, 124, And 125
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 6, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER's Motion To Compel Defendants' Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, Nos. 104, 105, 111, 118, 124, And 125 Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Request for Production, Set Two is granted as follows: Requests #104, 105, 124, 125: All objections are overruled. Defendant to produce documents responsive to these requests. Request #111: All objections are overruled except the objections on the grounds of confidential and/or proprietary. The documents are to be produced under a protective order and are only to be used for the purposes of the present litigation. All copies of these documents are to be returned to defendant within 90 days of the conclusion of the litigation and when all appeals have been exhausted. Request #118: All objections are overruled except for the objection regarding third party privacy rights. Defendant to produce the documents redacting all names, addresses, phone numbers of the employees. Defendant to provide a unique numerical identifier. Defendant to serve verified responses and produce all documents no later than January 23, 2015. If there are no further documents to produce, then the response shall so state. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: FRANK BROWN VS. MAUREEN BARBARA KENNEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF FRANK BROWN Motion For Leave To Amend First Amended Complaint. Granted. Plaintiff shall file his proposed Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") within 10 days of entry of this Order. The case is at its early stages (no answer has been filed, discovery has yet to commence, and no trial date has been set) and Defendants have not established they will be unduly prejudiced by the filing of the amended complaint. "The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified." (Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428.) Plaintiff's request to have the proposed SAC deemed filed and served is denied. Plaintiff must file and serve the SAC pursuant to statute and local rules. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
